response:
<reasoning>
The theorem is the standard `drop_append` lemma. It can be proved directly by induction on `n`, with the base case simplifying by `drop 0 = id`, and the successor case using the induction hypothesis on `xs` and `ys`.
</reasoning>

<used_lemmas>
none
</used_lemmas>

<lean4_proof>
induction n generalizing xs with
| zero =>
    simp
| succ n ih =>
    cases xs with
    | nil =>
        simp
    | cons x xs =>
        simp [ih]
</lean4_proof>
